data.title: Textron to separate industrial unit in shift to aerospace focus
data.description:
Textron (TXT) up 2.8% in early trading Thursday after reporting better-than-expected Q1 adjusted earnings and revenues and announcing plans to separate its industrial segment to enhance focus on its core aerospace and defense businesses.

Q1 profit edged up to $220M, or $1.25/share, from $207M, or $1.13/share, in the year-earlier quarter, and revenue rose 11.8% Y/Y to $3.69B, including a 22% revenue gain in Textron Aviation to $1.5B, supported by increased aircraft deliveries and stronger aftermarket services, and a 9% revenue increase to $1.1B at Bell, largely due to higher volume on the MV-75 Cheyenne program.

Textron (TXT) said it will explore options for the industrial unit, including a potential sale or tax-free spinoff into a standalone, publicly traded company.

The core part of the company is expected to generate more than $12B in revenue this year, while the industrial business, which is composed of the plastic fuel systems brand Kautex and Textron’s (TXT) specialized vehicles maker, is projected to amass $3B in revenue.

Textron (TXT) said it aims to complete the separation within 12-18 months.

data.title: ETFs tied to Caterpillar jump after the stocks earnings-driven rally
data.description:
Exchange traded funds with meaningful exposure to Caterpillar (CAT) moved into focus Thursday after the industrial heavyweight rallied sharply following its latest earnings release. Shares of Caterpillar climbed 6% in early trading, providing a notable lift to ETFs with concentrated allocations to the company.

In total, 445 ETFs have holdings in Caterpillar, collectively owning approximately 88.5M shares. This broad inclusion across both passive and active strategies underscores the company’s importance within industrial and large-cap U.S. equity benchmarks. As a result, price swings in Caterpillar often ripple across a wide range of funds, particularly those tied to industrials and cyclical growth themes.

With the stock advancing, ETFs with elevated Caterpillar weightings are likely to see near-term performance support, reinforcing the company’s role as a key driver within diversified portfolios. Listed below are the 10 funds with the largest allocations: 

  1. Global X Dow 30 Covered Call ETF (DJIA), 10.43% allocation.
  2. Global X Dow 30 Covered Call & Growth ETF (DYLG), 10.40% allocation.
  3. SPDR Dow Jones Industrial Average ETF Trust (DIA), 10.37% allocation.
  4. Inspire 100 ETF (BIBL), 8.53% allocation.
  5. ProShares Ultra Dow30 (DDM), 7.49% allocation. 
  6. Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLI), 7.17% allocation. 
  7. iShares Texas Equity ETF (TEXN), 7.05% allocation. 
  8. Castellan Targeted Income ETF (CTIF), 6.91% allocation. 
  9. VistaShares Target 15 USA Quality Income ETF (QUSA), 6.39% allocation. 
  10. Tema American Reshoring ETF (RSHO), 6.39% allocation.

data.title: Nauticus Robotics regains full Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• Nauticus Robotics (KITT) received formal confirmation from Nasdaq that it meets all continued listing requirements.
  • Compliance covers the period through the Nasdaq Hearing Panel jurisdiction, which ended April 14, 2026.
  • Nauticus must maintain minimum shareholders’ equity of $3.5 million each quarter during the monitoring period.
  • The Panel Monitor period runs until December 19, 2026.

data.title: AVITA Medical appoints Cary Vance as permanent CEO
data.description:
• AVITA Medical (RCEL) said on Thursday it appointed Cary Vance its CEO, effective immediately, after serving in the role on an interim basis since October 2025.
  • Vance will continue as a board member, while Jan Stern Reed was named chair of the board, also effective immediately.
  • The company said the appointment follows a formal search process. "The Board unanimously concluded that Cary is the right leader to serve as CEO on a permanent basis,” said newly appointed Chair Reed.
  • Vance, a healthcare executive with three decades of experience, said the company is focused on scaling adoption of its products and driving consistent performance as it builds momentum in the acute wound care market.

data.title: Establishment Labs secures $300M loan facility
data.description:
• Establishment Labs (ESTA) entered a $300M senior secured term loan with Oaktree Capital Management.
  • The facility refinances existing debt and extends maturity to 2031, improving financial flexibility.
  • The loan is split into $265M (refinancing tranche) and $35M (growth capital tranche).
  • The agreement features a fixed interest rate with potential reductions tied to leverage targets.
  • The deal supports positive free cash flow expected in H2 2026 and future expansion.

data.title: Wolfspeed appoints legal head and communications executive
data.description:
Wolfspeed (WOLF) has named Brad Kohn as its new Chief Legal and Global Affairs Officer and Sonja Burfeind as Vice President, Communications.

Kohn will join from May 11, overseeing global legal, compliance, government affairs, and public policy engagement functions. He has more than 20 years of experience as a general counsel across multiple public companies, including a previous stint at Wolfspeed.

Burfeind will join as VP on July 1, leading the silicon carbide technology company’s global communications strategy, including public relations and sales and marketing communications.

She has more than 20 years of experience across technology, automotive, consumer, and media industries. She last held senior communications leadership roles at Infineon (IFNNY) (IFNNF).

“These appointments reflect our continued investment in leadership that enhances Wolfspeed’s ability to engage effectively with customers, shareholders, and policymakers,” CEO Robert Feurle said. “Brad and Sonja bring deep expertise and proven results that will support our strategy and help amplify Wolfspeed’s influence globally.”
